title: feat: provide adaptive default radius and make rectangular elements' radii adjustable
body:
For rectangular elements (rectangles and diamonds), we provide three edge options:
- `sharp`
- `smart-default`
- `round`

For `smart-default`, users can draw rectangular elements whose radii will look more proportionate as the sizes of rectangular elements increase. This addresses some users' frustrations with how big the corner radii are for large rectangular elements if there were to have round corners. 

For `round`, this is to provide backwards compatibility, both in preserving the drawings and respecting users who are used to drawing with a fixed radius ratio. When `round`, an additional slider will show up allowing the users to configure what the radius ratio is. Previous default was `0.25`. Right now, a user will have the option to configure this ratio from the smallest possible ratio we allow to the maximum ratio (`0.5`).

title: fix: apply the right type of roundness when pasting styles
body:
Since diamonds only use proportional radius for now, we shouldn't copy adaptive radius from rectangles to diamonds, which unfortunately it does in production at the moment. 

While we don't wanna copy adaptive radius roundness from rectangles to diamonds, we do want the target diamonds to be rounded if styles are copied from a rounded rectangle.

So, what we are doing in this fix is to first check if the given source roundness type can be applied to the target element. If not, we provide a default roundness for the target element, so that the target element is rounded also.

title: feat: expose executeAction API to control actions externally
body:
While using excalidraw package we want to have more control over internal actions such are Undo/Redo, ZoomIn/ZoomOut, SaveToFile, etc externally. Instead of exposing implementation details such are `writeData`, `resaveAsImageWithScene`, etc I'm proposing to expose `executeAction` method which given access to `Action`s by `ActionName` provides an API to deal with actions.

It is also possible to expose `renderAction` instead/in addition but we don't always need an element to be rendered (e.g. custom keyboard shortcuts).

I'm also proposing to expose `redoOnce` and `undoOnce` required for `createRedoAction` and `createUndoAction` action builders respectively.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38251534/206879692-609e7612-d2a2-43f2-a2b6-3ef92782122e.mov

author: dwelle

title: fix: resize sometimes throwing on missing null-checks
body:
fix https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6009

Not quite fully fixed. We have a race condition in resizing where the resize handler holds onto an object that's no longer in the Scene elements array by the time it tries to retrieve the scene using the element.

But, it turns out this bug is not really an edge case and affects any sticky notes that are part of a group. As such it affects large swathes of libraries and user shapes, so we need to fix this asap.

This fix also seems to not exhibit and UX issues. That said, we need to figure out the root cause and fix it properly.

Also, how many times have we been burned by doing unnecessary type assertions? Let's stop doing that unless we're 99% certain it's safe. /cc @ad1992

title: fix: support breaking words containing hyphen -
body:
We currently treat words containing "-" as a single word but CSS wrapping algorithm allows breaking words containing "-" eg "non-profit" hence I have updated the algorithm to achieve the same behaviour

author: Jeffsingh

title: feat: move contextMenu into the component tree and control via appState
body:
fix https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5926

Turned out way more complex than anticipated, and this rewrite is still somewhat error-prone:

- since ContextMenu is now in the component tree, its items are re-evaluated on each rerender (while contextMenu is open). This means the item's `contextMenuPredicate()` (or `perform()` when executed) may throw if the state changed between open and render/confirm. This can happen if the user makes an action that doesn't close the contextMenu but changes selection (or other state the action works on).
  
  Current workaround:
  
  - filter out items based on their predicates on render, not state update (when `contextMenu.items` is populated)
  - close the contextMenu before selection is deselected on `pointerDown`
  
  What we should do instead:
  
  - [ ] make contextMenu (meaning Actions) operate on the state it was opened on, not on current state

- closing contextMenu is prone to race conditions. E.g., the contextMenu action user clicks on can update appState using the appState it's being passed, which still contains open `appState.contextMenu`.
  
  Current workaround:
  
  - wait until contextMenu is closed before executing contextMenu items
  
  What we should do instead:
  
  - Honestly, don't know. We can stop requiring actions to return fully qualified `appState` (by moving to latest TS and enabling [exactOptionalPropertyTypes](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/#exactOptionalPropertyTypes), but that won't enforce correct behavior). We can also ensure we reset the `contextMenu` on the `appState` that we pass to actions (but that will require support for passing custom `appState` when executing an action, as opposed to leaving it up to the manager. But if we do the previous suggestion of making contextMenu actions operate on initial state, then this will need to be done anyway).

- it also uncovers the issue of not having an easy way to subscribe on change to state/elements that should close the contextMenu, such as changing tools.

Alas, it will have to do for now and we'll improve it as we continue rewriting the editor flow.

----

- In this PR, we're now evaluating `contextItemPredicate` in place (render) instead of when setting the items, which simplifies things (and also fixes some things as per above).
- Separators are deduplicated instead of inlining the checks for it on open.
- Fixed the "right-clicking on common bounding box" doesn't open contextMenu as `element` type.
